2606.06379.title: EasyLens: A Training-Free Plug-and-Play Subtle-Lesion Representation Amplifier for Medical Vision-Language Models
2606.06379.paper_summary: Medical vision-language models (VLMs) have shown increasing potential for clinical image interpretation, including lesion detection and report generation. However, their practical utility remains limited by insufficient sensitivity to subtle lesions, whose visual evidence is often sparse, low-contrast, and embedded within complex anatomical context. As local visual tokens are aggregated, these weak lesion cues can become underrepresented in global image representations, making them difficult for medical VLMs to recognize. Existing efforts to improve lesion sensitivity mainly rely on medical-domain vision-encoder pre-training, clinical-term-guided alignment, or trainable pathological representation enhancement. Although effective, these approaches usually require additional training or model-specific adaptation and may overfit to particular disease morphologies, limiting their applicability to frozen medical VLMs. To address these limitations, we propose EasyLens, a training-free plug-and-play subtle-lesion representation amplifier for medical VLMs. EasyLens first constructs EasyBank, a pathology-anatomy prototype space that provides lesion-related prototypes and anatomy-aware normal references for comparing suspicious patches against both pathological and normal anatomical patterns. To avoid blindly amplifying normal tissues, EasyTag selects lesion-relevant patches through counterfactual prototype reasoning. To counteract the dilution of subtle lesion cues in global image representations, EasyAmplifier strengthens the selected lesion-relevant patch representations through morphology-guided residual enhancement, thereby increasing their contribution to the global image embedding. Experiments on multiple medical image datasets and frozen medical VLM backbones show that EasyLens improves subtle-lesion detection and outperforms existing encoder-enhancement baselines.
2606.06379.paper_summary_zh: 醫療視覺語言模型（VLMs）在臨床影像解釋方面顯示出越來越大的潛力，包括病變檢測和報告生成。<lb/>然而，它們的實用性仍然受到對微小病變敏感性不足的限制，這些病變的視覺證據通常稀疏、對比度低，並嵌入在複雜的解剖背景中。<lb/>隨著局部視覺標記的聚合，這些微弱的病變線索在全局影像表示中可能變得被低估，使得醫療VLMs難以識別。<lb/>現有的提高病變敏感性的努力主要依賴於醫療領域的視覺編碼器預訓練、臨床術語引導的對齊或可訓練的病理表示增強。<lb/>儘管有效，但這些方法通常需要額外的訓練或模型特定的適應，並可能過度擬合特定的疾病形態，限制了它們對凍結醫療VLMs的適用性。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了EasyLens，一種無需訓練的即插即用微小病變表示增強器，專為醫療VLMs設計。<lb/>EasyLens首先構建了EasyBank，一個病理-解剖原型空間，提供與病變相關的原型和具解剖意識的正常參考，以便將可疑區域與病理和正常解剖模式進行比較。<lb/>為了避免盲目放大正常組織，EasyTag通過反事實原型推理選擇與病變相關的區域。<lb/>為了對抗全局影像表示中微小病變線索的稀釋，EasyAmplifier通過形態引導的殘差增強來加強選定的與病變相關的區域表示，從而增加它們對全局影像嵌入的貢獻。<lb/>在多個醫療影像數據集和凍結醫療VLM骨幹上的實驗表明，EasyLens改善了微小病變檢測，並超越了現有的編碼器增強基準。
2606.06379.author: Qiwei Zeng et.al.
2606.06379.authors: Qiwei Zeng, Hao Wang, Jinghao Lin, Shuchang Ye, Yuezhe Yang, Yige Peng, Haoyuan Che, Jinman Kim, Lei Bi
